project.title: TEX264 coordinates p97- and SPRTN-mediated resolution of topoisomerase 1-DNA adducts
project.projectDescription: Eukaryotic topoisomerase 1 (TOP1) regulates DNA topology to ensure efficient DNA replication and transcription. TOP1 is also a major driver of endogenous genome instability, particularly when its catalytic intermediate - a covalent TOP1-DNA adduct known as a TOP1 cleavage complex (TOP1cc) - is stabilised.  TOP1ccs are highly cytotoxic and a failure to resolve them underlies the pathology of neurological disorders but is also exploited in cancer therapy where TOP1ccs are the target of widely used frontline anti-cancer drugs. A critical enzyme for TOP1cc resolution is the tyrosyl-DNA phosphodiesterase, TDP1, which hydrolyses the bond that links a tyrosine in the active site of TOP1 to a 3’ phosphate group on a single-stranded (ss)DNA break. However, TDP1 can only process small peptide fragments from ssDNA ends, raising the question of how the ~90 kDa TOP1 protein is processed upstream of TDP1. We find that TEX264 fulfils this role by forming a complex with the p97 ATPase and the SPRTN metalloprotease. We show that TEX264 recognises both unmodified and SUMO1-modifed TOP1 and initiates TOP1cc repair by recruiting p97 and SPRTN. TEX264 localises to the nuclear periphery, associates with DNA replication forks, and counteracts TOP1ccs during DNA replication. Altogether, our study elucidates the existence of a specialised repair complex required for upstream proteolysis of TOP1ccs and their subsequent resolution.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Eukaryotic topoisomerase 1 (TOP1) regulates DNA topology to ensure efficient DNA replication and transcription. TOP1 is also a major driver of endogenous genome instability, particularly when its catalytic intermediate - a covalent TOP1-DNA adduct known as a TOP1 cleavage complex (TOP1cc) - is stabilised. project.dataProcessingProtocol: Peptides and proteins were identified by searching the MS raw files against the Human SwissProt database downloaded in June 2017 (containing 20,206 sequences) for YFP-TOP1 and in Feb 2015 (20,274 sequences) for TEX264 pull down. The search was carried out by  enabling the Decoy function, whilst selecting trypsin as enzyme (allowing 1 missed cleavage), peptide charge of +2, +3, +4 ions, peptide tolerance of 10 ppm and MS/MS of 0.05 Da for Orbitrap Velos data) and of 0.5Da for Fusion Lumos data ; #13C at 1; Carboamidomethyl (C) as fixed modification; and Oxidation (M), Deamidation (NQ) and Acetylation on K and N-prot (only for Fusion lumos data) as a variable modification.MASCOT data outputs were filtered by applying a 20 ion cut off and 1% FDR above identity or homology threshold.

experimentTypes.name: Affinity purification coupled with mass spectrometry proteomics

references.referenceLine: Fielden J, Wiseman K, Torrecilla I, Li S, Hume S, Chiang SC, Ruggiano A, Narayan Singh A, Freire R, Hassanieh S, Domingo E, Vendrell I, Fischer R, Kessler BM, Maughan TS, El-Khamisy SF, Ramadan K. TEX264 coordinates p97- and SPRTN-mediated resolution of topoisomerase 1-DNA adducts. Nat Commun. 2020 11(1):1274

Characteristics[CellLine]: Flp-In TRex HEK293 cells inducibly expressing SSH-tagged TEX264
Characteristics[Organism]: Human HEK293, U2OS, RPE-1, and HeLa cells were obtained from ATCC
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: Trypsin was then added to a concentration of 200 ng/µL and incubated at 37 °C overnight
Characteristics[ReductionReagent]: reduced by addition of DTT to a final concentration of 5 mM
Characteristics[AlkylationReagent]: Iodoacetic acid (IAA) was then added to prevent to reformation of disulphide bridges to a final concentration of 20 mM
Comment[Instrument]: OrbiTrap LTQ Velos Elite Mass Spectrometer

Characteristics[Bait]: HEK293 cells were transfected with cDNA encoding YFP-tagged TOP1
Characteristics[Organism]: Human HEK293, U2OS, RPE-1, and HeLa cells were obtained from ATCC
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: digested with immobilised trypsin for 16 h at 37 °C
Comment[GradientTime]: one hour gradient of 2–35% acetonitrile in 5% dimethyl sulfoxide/0.1% formic acid (flow rate of 250 nl/min)
